task_completion.description: Set the observer location to Stonehenge
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Location window or the bottom status bar shows the coordinates approximately 51.1789° N, 1.8262° W (or 51°10'N, 1°49'W) and altitude ~102m.

task_completion.description: Set the date to June 21, 2500 BCE and time to 04:45 UTC
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Date/Time window or bottom status bar displays the year as -2499 (which is 2500 BCE in astronomical year numbering), month 6, day 21, and time around 04:45.

task_completion.description: Enable atmosphere, disable landscape, and enable both azimuthal and equatorial grids
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sky shows atmospheric scattering/glow, the ground/landscape is completely hidden (allowing visibility below the horizon), and two distinct sets of intersecting grid lines (azimuthal and equatorial) are visible on the sky.

task_completion.description: Center the Sun in the pre-dawn sky
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sun is visible and positioned in the center of the Stellarium viewport, likely highlighted with selection crosshairs.

task_completion.description: Capture a screenshot of the configured scene
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot notification in Stellarium (e.g., 'Screenshot saved...') or an image viewer showing the captured Stellarium scene.

A text editor or terminal window is open showing the creation or editing of '/home/ga/Desktop/stonehenge_alignment.txt' with content mentioning the location, date, and observations about the solstice sunrise.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Stellarium application
integrity.visual_evidence: Stellarium's authentic graphical user interface, including its toolbars, status bar, and rendering engine, should be visible in the screenshots.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually created and wrote to the text file
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano) or terminal commands (like echo or cat) should be visible showing the actual process of writing the file.

integrity.description: Verify the research notes contain actual observations rather than copied prompt text
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file should contain descriptive notes about the sun's position or alignment, not just a copy-paste of the task instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Location: Stonehenge, Wiltshire, UK (51.1789° N, 1.8262° W, 102m). Date: June 21, 2500 BCE. Note that in Stellarium, the year 2500 BCE is represented as -2499 due to the inclusion of a year 0 in astronomical year numbering. Time: 04:45:00 UTC. Required display flags: Atmosphere ON, Landscape OFF, Azimuthal Grid ON, Equatorial Grid ON. Output file: /home/ga/Desktop/stonehenge_alignment.txt.
